title: DVT & Pulmonary Embolism
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of DVT & Pulmonary Embolism
Identify key diagnostic criteria for DVT & Pulmonary Embolism
Recognize complications and when to escalate care

sections.title: Overview & Pathophysiology
sections.content: **DVT & Pulmonary Embolism** — This section covers the fundamental pathophysiology, epidemiology, and clinical significance. Understanding the underlying mechanisms is essential for clinical reasoning and evidence-based management.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation & Diagnosis
sections.content: Key clinical features, diagnostic criteria, and workup for DVT & Pulmonary Embolism. This section emphasizes pattern recognition and systematic diagnostic approaches.

sections.title: Management & Treatment
sections.content: Evidence-based management strategies for DVT & Pulmonary Embolism, including first-line therapies, escalation pathways, and monitoring parameters.

sections.title: Complications & Follow-Up
sections.content: Potential complications, prognosis, and longitudinal management considerations for DVT & Pulmonary Embolism.

keyTakeaways: Recognize the classic presentation of DVT & Pulmonary Embolism
Identify red flags requiring urgent intervention

references.title: CHEST Guideline: Antithrombotic Therapy for VTE

reviewQuestions.question: Which of the following is the MOST important initial step in evaluating a patient with suspected DVT & Pulmonary Embolism?
options: Obtain a thorough history and physical examination
Consult a specialist before any evaluation
reviewQuestions.explanation: A thorough history and physical examination is always the cornerstone of clinical evaluation for DVT & Pulmonary Embolism, guiding subsequent diagnostic workup and management decisions.

reviewQuestions.question: What is the MOST critical factor in determining the management approach for DVT & Pulmonary Embolism?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Clinical severity and appropriate risk stratification guide management decisions in DVT & Pulmonary Embolism, determining the level of intervention and monitoring required.

reviewQuestions.question: Which complication of DVT & Pulmonary Embolism requires the MOST urgent intervention?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Hemodynamic instability in the setting of DVT & Pulmonary Embolism requires immediate intervention including fluid resuscitation, close monitoring, and potentially escalation to critical care.

imaging_reasoning.order_reasoning: CTPA is gold standard after positive Wells/D-dimer; consider V/Q if contrast contraindicated
imaging_reasoning.expected_findings: Intraluminal filling defect, RV/LV ratio >1 indicates strain
imaging_reasoning.management_impact: Massive PE = thrombolytics; submassive = anticoagulation ± monitoring; saddle PE = ICU
